content:
[S1]: Vaccines are like tiny superhero training schools for your body’s immune system.  
[READER]: Wow, superhero training? That sounds fun! I want to know how my body can be like a superhero.  
[S2]: When you get a vaccine, it teaches your immune system to recognize the bad germs without making you sick.  
[READER]: Oh, so it’s like practice for my body? Cool! But how does it learn without getting sick?  
[S3]: The vaccine has small, safe pieces of the germ or a weakened form that can't hurt you but still show your body what to fight.  
[READER]: That makes sense! So my body sees the bad guy but it’s not dangerous? I like that idea.  
[S4]: Your immune system then makes special soldiers called antibodies to remember and fight the real germ if it ever appears.  
[READER]: Antibodies sound like real superheroes! I want to know what happens if the real germ shows up.  
[S5]: If the real germ tries to make you sick later, your immune system recognizes it quickly and stops it fast.  
[READER]: Oh, so the vaccine helps me stay healthy by getting ready in advance! That’s smart.  
[S6]: This is why vaccines help keep you and other people safe from getting really sick.  
[READER]: I like that vaccines protect not just me but my friends too. How do they share the protection?  
[S7]: When lots of people have vaccines, germs find it hard to spread, which helps protect everyone, even those who can’t get vaccines.  
[READER]: That sounds like teamwork! Vaccines help everyone stay safe together. I want to tell my friends!  

===BEGIN===  
Vaccines are like tiny superhero training schools for your body’s immune system.  
When you get a vaccine, it teaches your immune system to recognize the bad germs without making you sick.  
The vaccine has small, safe pieces of the germ or a weakened form that can't hurt you but still show your body what to fight.  
Your immune system then makes special soldiers called antibodies to remember and fight the real germ if it ever appears.  
If the real germ tries to make you sick later, your immune system recognizes it quickly and stops it fast.  
This is why vaccines help keep you and other people safe from getting really sick.  
When lots of people have vaccines, germs find it hard to spread, which helps protect everyone, even those who can’t get vaccines.  
That’s why vaccines are important for keeping you and your friends healthy!  
===END===
